steps.title: Show cause and result
steps.content: Typha latifolia absorbs and stores cadmium from polluted soil. The next clause says cadmium concentrations in the soil decrease, so the transition must show that this decrease follows from the plant's absorption.

steps.title: Reject noncausal transitions
steps.content: A would introduce a specific example, but the soil decrease is a result. C simply adds another fact and weakens the causal link. D signals contrast, the opposite of the relationship. The result transition fits the sequence. The answer is B.
